title: Tax Model Simplified
description: Simplified tax data model derived from tax-model-input-schema-annotated.json. Strips products, nativeAddress, and internal codes. Retains human_readable, description, valueType for insights.

summary.description: Indicates the primary filing status selected for the tax return, such as Single, Married Filing Jointly, etc.
Represents the deduction amount for qualified business income under Section 199A.

summary.human_readable: Tax on Certain Children's Unearned Income (Form 8615)
summary.description: Amount of tax calculated on a child's unearned income using IRS Form 8615.
Tax amount related to lump sum distributions, such as Social Security or retirement benefits.
Represents other miscellaneous federal taxes not categorized elsewhere on the return.
The calculated Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) liability for the taxpayer.
Credit amount for child and dependent care expenses paid to enable the taxpayer to work or look for work.
Total of education-related credits, such as the American Opportunity and Lifetime Learning credits.
The total Child Tax Credit claimed for qualifying children on the tax return.
Credit amount for qualified adoption expenses paid to adopt an eligible child.
Credit for mortgage interest paid or for DC first-time homebuyer credit, as applicable.
Credit for prior year minimum tax that can be used to offset regular tax liability.
Represents other miscellaneous credits not categorized elsewhere on the return.
The total self-employment tax calculated for the taxpayer, including Social Security and Medicare taxes.
Net Operating Loss (NOL) amount applied to reduce taxable income for the year.
The portion of Net Operating Loss (NOL) that was utilized in the current tax year.

summary.human_readable: Alternative Minimum Tax Net Operating Loss (AMT NOL)
summary.description: Net Operating Loss (NOL) amount applied for Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) purposes.

summary.human_readable: Additional Medicare Tax Net Operating Loss
summary.description: Net Operating Loss (NOL) amount applied to the Additional Medicare Tax calculation.
Net Operating Loss (NOL) amount related to deductions carried forward or back.
Net Operating Loss (NOL) amount applied to self-employment tax calculations.
Indicates whether the taxpayer lived with their spouse at any time during the tax year.
Adjustment amount for state tax withholding attributed to the taxpayer.
Adjustment amount for state tax withholding attributed to the spouse.

summary.human_readable: Marginal New York City Tax Rate
summary.description: The marginal tax rate applied to income for New York City residents.
Input value for calculating the residential energy credit for qualified home improvements.
Adjustment amount added to state taxable income for state tax purposes.
Adjustment amount subtracted from state taxable income for state tax purposes.
Indicates whether the taxpayer has made a Section 475 election for mark-to-market accounting.
Indicator or flag related to capital gains treatment or reporting.
The tax rate applied to a specific tax planning strategy or scenario.
The Social Security Number (SSN) of the dependent, used for identification and tax purposes.
The date of birth of the dependent.
The state in which the dependent was born.
Describes the dependent's relationship to the taxpayer (e.g., child, parent, other).
Indicates the type or category of the dependent (e.g., qualifying child, qualifying relative).
The first name of the dependent as reported on the tax return.
The last name (surname) of the dependent as reported on the tax return.
Indicates whether the taxpayer is claiming this individual as a dependent on the tax return.
The amount of health insurance premiums paid by a self-employed individual, eligible for deduction.
The amount contributed to an Individual Retirement Account (IRA) for the tax year.
The first name of the taxpayer as reported on the tax return.
The last name (surname) of the taxpayer as reported on the tax return.
The date of birth of the taxpayer.
The state in which the taxpayer primarily resided during the tax year.
The taxpayer's filing status for the tax year (e.g., Single, Married Filing Jointly, etc.).
The year in which the taxpayer's spouse passed away, if applicable.
The amount of student loan interest paid by the taxpayer, which may be deductible.
Indicator to force the taxpayer to be treated as a real estate professional for tax purposes.
Adjustment amount related to rental income or expenses.
Adjustment to self-employment income not related to farming activities.
The amount contributed to an Individual Retirement Account (IRA) by the spouse for the tax year.
The first name of the spouse as reported on the tax return.
The last name (surname) of the spouse as reported on the tax return.

summary.human_readable: Spouse Force Real Estate Professional Status
summary.description: Indicator to force the spouse to be treated as a real estate professional for tax purposes.

summary.human_readable: Spouse Self-Employment Non-Farm Income Adjustment Code
summary.description: Adjustment code for the spouse's self-employment income not related to farming activities.
